metadata.climate_notes: Mild temperate oceanic - frost rare, moderate rainfall year-round, humidity can be high
metadata.focus: Storage staples and pest/disease resistant greens

vegetables.storage_method: Cool, dark, ventilated area (7-10°C). Do not refrigerate.
vegetables.growing_notes: Mound soil around stems as plants grow (earthing up). Plant seed potatoes 10cm deep. Avoid planting where tomatoes or peppers grew recently.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Rocket (early, blight resistant), Desiree (red, good storage), Agria (great for chips), Jersey Benne (NZ heritage)
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Tomato-potato psyllid is the main Auckland pest — use exclusion netting. Late blight can occur in wet weather; choose resistant varieties.

vegetables.storage_method: Cure at 25-30°C for 10 days with high humidity, then store at 13-15°C in a dark ventilated area.
vegetables.growing_notes: Grow from kumara tips (sprouts) not seed. Needs warm soil — plant after last frost. Can sprawl or train up a trellis. Very well suited to Auckland climate.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Owairaka Red (NZ standard), Beauregard (American, fast growing), Toka Toka Gold
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Very few pest issues in Auckland. Occasional caterpillar damage on leaves. Cure properly after harvest to prevent rot.

vegetables.soil_type: fertile, well-draining, rich in organic matter
vegetables.storage_method: Dry thoroughly in sun or airy shed for 2-3 weeks. Store in mesh bags in cool, dry, dark place.
vegetables.growing_notes: Short-day varieties suit Auckland. Can grow from seed or sets. Stop watering when tops start to fall over.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Pukekohe Long Keeper (NZ heritage, excellent storage), Californian Red, Creamgold
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Onion rust can be an issue in humid weather — ensure good air circulation. Thrips may cause silvering of leaves.

vegetables.soil_type: fertile, well-draining, rich in organic matter
vegetables.storage_method: Cure in airy, dry location for 2-3 weeks. Braid or hang in mesh bags. Stores 6+ months easily.
vegetables.growing_notes: Plant individual cloves pointed end up. Mulch well. Remove scapes (flower stalks) to increase bulb size. Needs a cold period to form bulbs.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Printanor (softneck, great storage), elephant garlic (mild, huge bulbs), Yen Ben (NZ standard hardneck)
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Very few problems. White rot can persist in soil — rotate crops. Rust occasionally in humid weather.

vegetables.soil_type: deep, loose, well-draining, stone-free sandy loam
vegetables.storage_method: Remove tops, store in fridge in damp sand or paper bags. Can also leave in ground and harvest as needed.
vegetables.growing_notes: Sow direct — carrots dislike transplanting. Thin to 5cm spacing. Keep soil consistently moist for germination. Deep, loose soil prevents forking.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Nantes (quick, sweet), Autumn King (large, stores well), Chantenay (short, heavy soils), Cosmic Purple
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Carrot rust fly is the main pest — use fine mesh coverings. Avoid thinning in the evening when flies are most active.

vegetables.storage_method: Cure in sun for 10 days after harvest. Store in cool, dry place (10-15°C). Handle carefully to avoid bruising.
vegetables.growing_notes: Very heavy feeders — add lots of compost. Train vines to save space or let sprawl. Lift fruit off soil with a tile to prevent rot.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Crown (the classic NZ pumpkin), Butternut (see separate entry), Triamble (heirloom, ornamental), Ironbark
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Powdery mildew common in Auckland humidity. Spray with milk solution (1:9 milk:water). Protect young plants from slugs.

vegetables.storage_method: Cure in sun for 10 days. Store at 10-15°C in dry conditions.
vegetables.growing_notes: More compact than Crown pumpkin. Excellent flavour and versatile in the kitchen. Can train vertically on strong trellis.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Butternut (Waltham), Butterbush (compact), Tromboncino (climbing)
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Similar to pumpkin — powdery mildew is the main issue. Aphids can transmit viruses; control early.

vegetables.storage_method: Remove tops, store in fridge or damp sand in a cool place. Leaves also edible.
vegetables.growing_notes: Each 'seed' is actually a cluster — thin to 10cm. Sow every 3-4 weeks for continuous harvest. Tolerates partial shade. Leaves are excellent in salads.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Detroit Dark Red (reliable), Chioggia (candy-striped), Golden (mild), Boltardy (slow to bolt)
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Very few pest issues in Auckland. Leaf miner occasionally — remove affected leaves. Generally trouble-free.

vegetables.storage_method: Leave in ground and harvest as needed, or store in damp sand in a cool place.
vegetables.growing_notes: Seed is short-lived — use fresh seed each year. Slow germination is normal (up to 4 weeks). Frost improves flavour — sweeter after cold weather.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Hollow Crown (reliable), Avonresister (canker resistant), Gladiator F1 (hybrid)
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Canker can affect roots in heavy soil — grow in deep, loose soil. Very few pest issues otherwise.

vegetables.soil_type: fertile, well-draining, rich in organic matter
vegetables.storage_method: Store in fridge wrapped in damp cloth. Can also leave in ground and harvest as needed.
vegetables.growing_notes: Transplant into 15cm deep holes (trench method). Soil gradually fills in around stems as they grow. Long season crop but easy.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Musselburgh (large, hardy), Giant Winter, King Richard (early, slim)
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Very few problems. Rust occasionally in humid weather. Generally one of the most trouble-free vegetables to grow.

vegetables.storage_method: Dry pods on plant or in airy shed. Shell when fully dry. Store in airtight containers in cool, dark place. Lasts years.
vegetables.growing_notes: Let pods dry completely on the plant before harvesting. Fix nitrogen in soil — good rotation crop. Include climbing and bush types.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Borlotti (Italian, speckled), Cannellini, Black Turtle, Navy beans, Scarlet Runner (dual purpose — eat fresh or dry)
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Mexican bean beetle can be an issue — hand-pick. Avoid overhead watering to prevent fungal issues.

vegetables.storage_method: Store in fridge crisper or in damp sand in a cool shed. Remove leaves before storing.
vegetables.growing_notes: Needs consistent moisture. Slow to germinate — start in trays. Trim side roots and remove lower leaves to encourage swelling. Excellent in soups and remoulade.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Giant Prague (classic), Monarch (smooth), Ibis F1 (resists bolting)
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Celery leaf miner occasionally — remove affected leaves. Slugs like moisture — mulch carefully. Generally very low maintenance.

vegetables.storage_method: Harvest fresh and use within a week. Store in fridge crisper.
vegetables.growing_notes: Year-round harvest in Auckland. Pick outer leaves and let the centre keep growing. One of the most reliable and productive greens. Biennial — will flower in year 2.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Fordhook Giant (classic, vigorous), Rainbow Chard (colourful stems), Rhubarb Chard (red stems)
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Virtually pest-free. Slugs occasionally on young plants. Leaf miner very rarely. One of the most trouble-free greens you can grow.

vegetables.storage_method: Harvest fresh. Stores in fridge up to a week. Can also freeze after blanching.
vegetables.growing_notes: Frost improves flavour (sweeter after cold). Pick outer leaves for continuous harvest. Curly types tend to be more pest resistant than flat-leaf. Excellent nutrition.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Dwarf Green Curled (hardy, pest resistant), Red Russian (mild, flat leaf), Tuscan Black/Cavolo Nero (see separate), Walking Stick
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: White butterfly caterpillars are the main pest — use fine mesh/netting. Curly varieties are harder for butterflies to lay eggs on. Cabbage aphid occasionally.

vegetables.storage_method: Best eaten fresh on day of harvest. Rinse and store in damp paper towel in fridge for 2-3 days.
vegetables.growing_notes: One of the fastest and easiest greens. Sow every 2-3 weeks for continuous supply. Bolts in hot weather — partial shade extends the season. Self-seeds readily.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Wild Rocket (pungent, serrated leaves), Salad Rocket (milder, smooth), Astro
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Very pest resistant — the strong flavour deters most insects. Flea beetle occasionally but rarely damaging. Essentially trouble-free.

vegetables.storage_method: Harvest fresh. Store in fridge crisper for up to a week.
vegetables.growing_notes: Very fast growing — harvest in 4-6 weeks. Peppery flavour. Sow successionally every 3 weeks. Excellent as a green manure if left to flower.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Red Giant (purple leaves, mild), Green Wave (frilly, hot), Mizuna (see separate entry), Tendergreen (mild)
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Strong flavour repels most pests. White butterfly may try but mustard grows fast enough to outpace damage. Excellent pest resistance.

vegetables.storage_method: Harvest fresh. Stores in fridge for up to a week.
vegetables.growing_notes: Actually a chard bred for leaf production. Much hardier than true spinach — resists bolting in Auckland heat. Pick outer leaves for continuous harvest. Biennial.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Perpetual Spinach / Spinach Beet (standard), Lucullus
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Same excellent pest resistance as silverbeet. Virtually no pest or disease issues. Superior to true spinach in Auckland's climate.

vegetables.soil_type: any reasonable soil, well-draining, tolerates poor soil
vegetables.storage_method: Harvest fresh. Blanches and freezes well.
vegetables.growing_notes: Native relative — bred for edible leaves. Heat tolerant unlike true spinach. Trailing habit — good ground cover or over a wall. Pick tips to encourage bushiness. Not related to true spinach.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Standard NZ Spinach (the species — no named varieties needed)
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Essentially pest-free. The slightly fuzzy leaves deter insects. One of the most resilient greens for Auckland summers.

vegetables.storage_method: Harvest fresh. Stores in fridge crisper for 3-5 days.
vegetables.growing_notes: Japanese green. Mild mustard flavour. Harvest as baby leaf in 21 days or full size in 35 days. Cut-and-come-again. Excellent in stir-fries and salads.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Mibuna (standard — no named varieties needed)
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Strong deters most pests. Very rarely bothered by white butterfly. Fast growth outpaces any minor damage.

vegetables.storage_method: Harvest fresh. Stores in fridge for 3-5 days.
vegetables.growing_notes: Japanese green. Feathery, serrated leaves. Mild peppery flavour. Very productive — one plant gives continuous harvests. Self-seeds readily in Auckland.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Green Mizuna (standard), Red Mizuna (purple tinged), Tokyo Bekana (similar, very mild)
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Excellent pest resistance. Very rarely affected by white butterfly. Fast growing and resilient.

vegetables.soil_type: fertile, well-draining, rich in organic matter
vegetables.storage_method: Harvest fresh. Stores in fridge up to a week. Excellent for freezing.
vegetables.growing_notes: Tuscan kale. Dramatic dark blue-green crinkled leaves. Frost improves sweetness. Tall plants — up to 1m. Harvest lower leaves first. Essential for Italian cooking (ribollita).
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Nero di Toscana (authentic), Black Magic (compact), Dazzling Blue (cold tolerant)
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: White butterfly caterpillars are the main pest — use fine mesh netting. More pest resistant than regular cabbage. Can be grown successfully with netting.

vegetables.storage_method: Harvest fresh. Stores in fridge crisper for up to a week.
vegetables.growing_notes: More heat tolerant and pest resistant than butterhead types. Can harvest outer leaves or whole head. Succession sow every 2-3 weeks.
vegetables.auckland_varieties: Parris Island Cos (classic romaine), Little Gem (compact, sweet), Freckles (speckled, heirloom)
vegetables.pest_disease_notes: Slugs and snails are the main pest — use beer traps or copper tape. Lettuce root aphid can occur; rotate crops. More pest resistant than soft lettuces.
